response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer with strong testing discipline and some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments, but the role requires deep embedded LAN-switch firmware expertise, Linux kernel modification, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), and native-level Japanese — none of which are evidenced in the resume. The mismatch on language requirement alone is disqualifying, and there is no demonstrated background in networking stacks, switch chips, or embedded security.
strengths: Broad polyglot experience including a stint writing C/C++ for NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, Linux familiarity
Long track record of shipping production systems across web, mobile, and embedded-adjacent domains
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency indicated (hard requirement)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, MACsec/802.1X, or Linux kernel modification experience
Career trajectory is web/cloud/TypeScript-focused, not low-level C on embedded networking hardware

key_factors.factor: Some prior C/C++ and Linux background
